agent_output: {"handoff_brief":{"recipient":"Shaq","task_context":"Build the Order Fulfillment state management system. Bird has defined the domain invariants governing order state transitions and refund eligibility. MJ has designed the technical architecture: a PATCH endpoint for state changes, an OrderStateMachine for transition validation, and a separate RefundService that verifies order state before issuing refunds. Your job is to implement all three components with the domain rules baked in.","domain_rules":[{"rule":"An order cannot transition from SHIPPED to PENDING","testable_assertion":"Calling transitionState(SHIPPED, PENDING) on OrderStateMachine must throw/return ORDER_STATE_INVALID"},{"rule":"A refund can only be issued on a DELIVERED order","testable_assertion":"RefundService.issueRefund(orderId) must reject with an error when the order's current state is anything other than DELIVERED"}],"architecture_guidance":[{"decision":"Expose a PATCH /orders/{id}/state endpoint for all state transitions","implementation_note":"The endpoint receives the target state in the request body. It delegates to OrderStateMachine for validation before persisting. Return 409 Conflict with the appropriate error code when a transition is rejected."},{"decision":"State transition validation lives in an OrderStateMachine class","implementation_note":"This is the single source of truth for which transitions are legal. Encode allowed transitions explicitly (whitelist approach) rather than blacklisting invalid ones. This makes the invariant 'SHIPPED cannot go to PENDING' fall out naturally — it simply is not in the allowed set."},{"decision":"Refund logic lives in a separate RefundService that calls the order service to verify state","implementation_note":"RefundService must query the order's current state (via the order service, not its own DB read) before issuing a refund. This keeps the order state ownership in one place. If the order is not in DELIVERED state, RefundService must abort and return a clear error — do not issue a refund and then try to roll back."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"criterion":"Invalid state transition SHIPPED to PENDING is rejected","given":"An order in SHIPPED state","when":"A PATCH /orders/{id}/state request attempts to set state to PENDING","then":"The request is rejected with HTTP 409 and error code ORDER_STATE_INVALID"},{"criterion":"Refund is only issued on DELIVERED orders","given":"An order in any state other than DELIVERED","when":"RefundService.issueRefund is called for that order","then":"The refund is rejected with a clear error indicating the order is not in a refundable state"},{"criterion":"Refund succeeds on DELIVERED order","given":"An order in DELIVERED state","when":"RefundService.issueRefund is called for that order","then":"The refund is issued successfully"},{"criterion":"Valid state transitions are accepted","given":"An order in a state with a legal transition to the target state","when":"A PATCH /orders/{id}/state request is made with the valid target state","then":"The order state is updated and HTTP 200 is returned with the updated order"}],"terminology_alignment":[{"domain_term":"Order state invariant","technical_term":"Transition validation rule in OrderStateMachine","definition":"A business rule that restricts which state transitions are permitted. Enforced at the domain layer (OrderStateMachine), surfaced at the API layer as error codes."},{"domain_term":"ORDER_STATE_INVALID","technical_term":"409 Conflict response with error code","definition":"The error code returned when a requested state transition violates a domain invariant. Maps to HTTP 409 at the API boundary."}],"contradictions_resolved":[],"open_questions":["What are the complete set of valid order states beyond PENDING, SHIPPED, and DELIVERED? Bird defined invariants for these three — Shaq should confirm whether additional states (e.g., CANCELLED, PROCESSING, RETURNED) are in scope.","Should RefundService call the order service over HTTP or via an in-process dependency? MJ says 'calls the order service' — clarify if this implies a network call or a direct class reference."]},"escalations":[],"confidence":{"level":85,"high_confidence_areas":["Domain invariants are clear and directly testable","Architecture decomposition is well-defined with clean separation of concerns","Bird and MJ outputs are fully consistent — no contradictions"],"low_confidence_areas":["Complete list of valid order states and transitions is not fully enumerated","Communication mechanism between RefundService and order service (HTTP vs in-process) is ambiguous"],"assumptions":["The OrderStateMachine uses a whitelist of allowed transitions rather than a blacklist of forbidden ones","ORDER_STATE_INVALID is the canonical error code for all invalid transitions, not just SHIPPED-to-PENDING","RefundService is the only consumer of order state checks for refund eligibility (no other service bypasses it)"]}}
agent_output_excerpt: {"handoff_brief":{"recipient":"Shaq","task_context":"Build the Order Fulfillment state management system. Bird has defined the domain invariants governing order state transitions and refund eligibility. MJ has designed the technical architecture: a PATCH endpoint for state changes, an OrderStateMachine for transition validation, and a separate RefundService that verifies order state before issuing refunds. Your job is to implement all three components with the domain rules baked in.","domain_rul

content.thinking: Straightforward synthesis task with consistent inputs from Bird and MJ. No contradictions detected.
